title: pelargonium × eriocaulon
intro: Pelargonium × eriocaulon, also known as Rose Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the family Geraniaceae, resulting from the cross between Pelargonium tomentosum and Pelargonium capitatum. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and other conditions. The health benefits of Rose Geranium include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, among others. Rose Geranium is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and tinctures, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
